channel.title: Stillwater Civic Briefing
channel.description: Local government and schools across the Stillwater–Oakdale area of Minnesota — Washington County, Stillwater, Oak Park Heights, Bayport, Lake Elmo, Oakdale, Woodbury, and the local school districts. Three briefings a week: the week's news on Monday, a profile of a public figure on Wednesday, and a deeper look at one development on Friday. Just the facts — what was decided, what it does, what it costs, and who's responsible.
channel.itunes:author: Stillwater Civic Briefing
channel.itunes:summary: Local government and schools across the Stillwater–Oakdale area of Minnesota — Washington County, Stillwater, Oak Park Heights, Bayport, Lake Elmo, Oakdale, Woodbury, and the local school districts. Three briefings a week: the week's news on Monday, a profile of a public figure on Wednesday, and a deeper look at one development on Friday. Just the facts — what was decided, what it does, what it costs, and who's responsible.

item.title: Week of July 13, 2026: County Road-and-Design Money, Woodbury's Bailey West, and Oakdale's PFAS Grants
item.description:
This week's civic news across the Stillwater–Oakdale area: Washington County lines up road and engineering money at its July 7 board meeting; Woodbury holds a public hearing on the Bailey West rezoning and plat; Lake Elmo receives its 2025 audit and keeps its street projects moving; and Oakdale prepares to accept nearly $2.4 million in state grants to keep treating its drinking water for PFAS.
WHAT HAPPENED THIS WEEK - Washington County Board (Tue, July 7): a $2,280,159 engineering/design contract with Short Elliott Hendrickson for the County Road 19A / 100th Street realignment; budget amendments (about $408,000 and $1,458,000, largely from the county's turnback fund) plus a $407,850 Kimley-Horn design amendment and a right-of-way resolution for the Century North reconstruction; and 8.0 new full-time positions in Community Services (access, aging, disability). Context: in June the board awarded a ~$27.3 million construction contract to Ames Construction for the Lake Elmo Avenue / Highway 36 intersection. - Woodbury City Council (Wed, July 8): a public hearing on the Bailey West project — a comprehensive-plan change, a rezoning out of Business Campus District into R-4 Urban Residential and B-3 Planned Shopping Center (Ordinance 2080), and preliminary/final plat approval; the Planning Commission had recommended approval 6-0. The ~185-acre master plan also makes room for New Life Academy, which is acquiring a parcel to expand its athletic and recreation fields. Also: setting a hearing on the 2027 street reconstruction, a $104,994 Metropolitan Council sewer grant, new cardiac monitors, election judges for the Aug. 11 primary, and about $7.8 million in bills. - Lake Elmo City Council (Tue, July 7): presented the city's 2025 audited financial statements; first amendments to the development agreements for the Lake Elmo Elementary and Oak Land Middle School sites; the 2026 seal-coat and striping contract awards; a pay request on new Test Well 6; a full-time firefighter hire; election judges; and a first cooperative payment toward the Highway 36 / Lake Elmo Avenue interchange. - Oakdale Planning Commission (Thu, July 9): changes to the Oakdale Marketplace planned development, including dropping a restaurant requirement, forwarded to the City Council. - Oakdale (on the July 14 council agenda): accept $1,929,000 from the MPCA and DNR to operate and maintain PFAS filtration on wells 5, 7, and 9; $480,000 for water-treatment-plant planning and design; and bidding work for two new wells (11 and 12). Staff tie the work to 3M PFAS settlement money.
HOW TO GET INVOLVED / THE WEEK AHEAD - Washington County Board: Tue, July 14, 9:00 AM, County Board Room, Government Center, 14949 62nd St N, Stillwater. Agenda includes a $1,200,000 state homeless-prevention grant (FHPAP), a Safe Routes to School project near Grant and the Mahtomedi schools (a $381,529.77 contract with Dresel Contracting), and a Private Well Protection Grant application. Public comment period; a comment card is filled out before the meeting. - Oakdale City Council: Tue, July 14, 7:00 PM, Oakdale City Hall, 1584 Hadley Ave N — the PFAS water grants, a new Tower Park playground, and about $5 million in claims. - Lake Elmo City Council: special meeting and workshop, Tue, July 14, 3880 Laverne Ave N. - Stillwater: Heritage Preservation Commission, Wed, July 15; City Council, Tue, July 21, 7:00 PM, Stillwater City Hall; Planning Commission, July 22; Parks & Recreation Commission, July 27. - Minnesota statewide primary election: Tue, Aug. 11. Cities across the area appointed election judges this week.
Sources: official meeting agendas, packets, and staff reports from Washington County, and the cities of Woodbury, Lake Elmo, and Oakdale, plus the City of Stillwater's public meeting calendar. Approved minutes for this week's July 7–9 meetings were not yet posted at publication, so outcomes are reported as agenda business rather than final votes, except where a body's own recorded recommendation is cited.
item.itunes:summary:
This week's civic news across the Stillwater–Oakdale area: Washington County lines up road and engineering money at its July 7 board meeting; Woodbury holds a public hearing on the Bailey West rezoning and plat; Lake Elmo receives its 2025 audit and keeps its street projects moving; and Oakdale prepares to accept nearly $2.4 million in state grants to keep treating its drinking water for PFAS.
WHAT HAPPENED THIS WEEK - Washington County Board (Tue, July 7): a $2,280,159 engineering/design contract with Short Elliott Hendrickson for the County Road 19A / 100th Street realignment; budget amendments (about $408,000 and $1,458,000, largely from the county's turnback fund) plus a $407,850 Kimley-Horn design amendment and a right-of-way resolution for the Century North reconstruction; and 8.0 new full-time positions in Community Services (access, aging, disability). Context: in June the board awarded a ~$27.3 million construction contract to Ames Construction for the Lake Elmo Avenue / Highway 36 intersection. - Woodbury City Council (Wed, July 8): a public hearing on the Bailey West project — a comprehensive-plan change, a rezoning out of Business Campus District into R-4 Urban Residential and B-3 Planned Shopping Center (Ordinance 2080), and preliminary/final plat approval; the Planning Commission had recommended approval 6-0. The ~185-acre master plan also makes room for New Life Academy, which is acquiring a parcel to expand its athletic and recreation fields. Also: setting a hearing on the 2027 street reconstruction, a $104,994 Metropolitan Council sewer grant, new cardiac monitors, election judges for the Aug. 11 primary, and about $7.8 million in bills. - Lake Elmo City Council (Tue, July 7): presented the city's 2025 audited financial statements; first amendments to the development agreements for the Lake Elmo Elementary and Oak Land Middle School sites; the 2026 seal-coat and striping contract awards; a pay request on new Test Well 6; a full-time firefighter hire; election judges; and a first cooperative payment toward the Highway 36 / Lake Elmo Avenue interchange. - Oakdale Planning Commission (Thu, July 9): changes to the Oakdale Marketplace planned development, including dropping a restaurant requirement, forwarded to the City Council. - Oakdale (on the July 14 council agenda): accept $1,929,000 from the MPCA and DNR to operate and maintain PFAS filtration on wells 5, 7, and 9; $480,000 for water-treatment-plant planning and design; and bidding work for two new wells (11 and 12). Staff tie the work to 3M PFAS settlement money.
HOW TO GET INVOLVED / THE WEEK AHEAD - Washington County Board: Tue, July 14, 9:00 AM, County Board Room, Government Center, 14949 62nd St N, Stillwater. Agenda includes a $1,200,000 state homeless-prevention grant (FHPAP), a Safe Routes to School project near Grant and the Mahtomedi schools (a $381,529.77 contract with Dresel Contracting), and a Private Well Protection Grant application. Public comment period; a comment card is filled out before the meeting. - Oakdale City Council: Tue, July 14, 7:00 PM, Oakdale City Hall, 1584 Hadley Ave N — the PFAS water grants, a new Tower Park playground, and about $5 million in claims. - Lake Elmo City Council: special meeting and workshop, Tue, July 14, 3880 Laverne Ave N. - Stillwater: Heritage Preservation Commission, Wed, July 15; City Council, Tue, July 21, 7:00 PM, Stillwater City Hall; Planning Commission, July 22; Parks & Recreation Commission, July 27. - Minnesota statewide primary election: Tue, Aug. 11. Cities across the area appointed election judges this week.
Sources: official meeting agendas, packets, and staff reports from Washington County, and the cities of Woodbury, Lake Elmo, and Oakdale, plus the City of Stillwater's public meeting calendar. Approved minutes for this week's July 7–9 meetings were not yet posted at publication, so outcomes are reported as agenda business rather than final votes, except where a body's own recorded recommendation is cited.

item.title: Profile: Ted Kozlowski, Mayor of Stillwater
item.description:
A factual portrait of Ted Kozlowski, mayor of Stillwater since 2014, drawn from the public record and his own words. Covers his upbringing and digital-marketing career, Stillwater's mayor-council-administrator form of government, his 2012 council election and his 2014, 2018, and 2022 mayoral elections (with margins), and his record on the downtown riverfront and the St. Croix Crossing project, quoted from his own public statements.
Sources: 2014 Stillwater mayoral election coverage, Stillwater Gazette / hometownsource.com ("Kozlowski will be the 50th mayor of Stillwater" and 2022 local election results); 2022 election totals and Stillwater city-government structure via Ballotpedia and the City of Stillwater; Chestnut Street Plaza remarks quoted from KSTP (2023); career background from DemandQuest / the mayor's own published biography; council roster and City Administrator Joe Kohlmann via the City of Stillwater. His Japan (2023) and Taiwan (2019) delegate roles are attributed to his own published biography.
item.itunes:summary:
A factual portrait of Ted Kozlowski, mayor of Stillwater since 2014, drawn from the public record and his own words. Covers his upbringing and digital-marketing career, Stillwater's mayor-council-administrator form of government, his 2012 council election and his 2014, 2018, and 2022 mayoral elections (with margins), and his record on the downtown riverfront and the St. Croix Crossing project, quoted from his own public statements.
Sources: 2014 Stillwater mayoral election coverage, Stillwater Gazette / hometownsource.com ("Kozlowski will be the 50th mayor of Stillwater" and 2022 local election results); 2022 election totals and Stillwater city-government structure via Ballotpedia and the City of Stillwater; Chestnut Street Plaza remarks quoted from KSTP (2023); career background from DemandQuest / the mayor's own published biography; council roster and City Administrator Joe Kohlmann via the City of Stillwater. His Japan (2023) and Taiwan (2019) delegate roles are attributed to his own published biography.

item.title: Week of July 6, 2026: County Road Money, Stillwater's Packet, and Two New Cities
item.description:
Going deeper this week: Washington County votes on ~$4 million in road work (July 7); Stillwater's council packet has a new Parks Master Plan, two paving contracts to Miller Excavating, a water-tower painting change order now at ~$642K (tied to the city's PFAS response), and a school resource officer renewal; Lake Elmo holds a closed session on threatened litigation and receives its 2025 audit; and Woodbury weighs the Bailey West rezoning/plat at a public hearing. Plus Oakdale's City Hall rebuild and the Stillwater district's sports-fee and class-size changes.
HOW TO GET INVOLVED - Washington County Board: Tue, July 7, 9:00 AM, County Board Room, Government Center, Stillwater. Public comment period; fill out a comment card before the meeting. - Stillwater City Council: Tue, July 7, workshop 4:30 PM / regular 7:00 PM, Stillwater City Hall. - Lake Elmo City Council: Tue, July 7, closed session 6:00 PM / regular 7:00 PM, 3880 Laverne Ave N. - Woodbury City Council: Wed, July 8, 7:30 PM, 8301 Valley Creek Rd — includes the Bailey West public hearing. - Oakdale Planning Commission: Thu, July 9; two public hearings scheduled. - County online comment windows (washingtoncountymn.gov): proposed Middle St. Croix Valley-to-Phalen-Keller regional trail closes July 10; County Road 10/17 intersection near Lake Elmo closes July 12. - Stillwater middle-school sports fee: the final amount is set before mid-July registration; watch the district's Community Education page. - Stuff the Bus school-supply help: register through July 23 at uwwce.org/stuff-the-bus. - Also coming up: Woodbury 2027 road-reconstruction public hearing July 22; County Board and Oakdale City Council July 14; Stillwater City Council July 21; Stillwater school board July 28.
Hosted by Artie Fishel. Sources: official city, county, and school-district agendas, meeting packets, and staff reports.
item.itunes:summary:
Going deeper this week: Washington County votes on ~$4 million in road work (July 7); Stillwater's council packet has a new Parks Master Plan, two paving contracts to Miller Excavating, a water-tower painting change order now at ~$642K (tied to the city's PFAS response), and a school resource officer renewal; Lake Elmo holds a closed session on threatened litigation and receives its 2025 audit; and Woodbury weighs the Bailey West rezoning/plat at a public hearing. Plus Oakdale's City Hall rebuild and the Stillwater district's sports-fee and class-size changes.
HOW TO GET INVOLVED - Washington County Board: Tue, July 7, 9:00 AM, County Board Room, Government Center, Stillwater. Public comment period; fill out a comment card before the meeting. - Stillwater City Council: Tue, July 7, workshop 4:30 PM / regular 7:00 PM, Stillwater City Hall. - Lake Elmo City Council: Tue, July 7, closed session 6:00 PM / regular 7:00 PM, 3880 Laverne Ave N. - Woodbury City Council: Wed, July 8, 7:30 PM, 8301 Valley Creek Rd — includes the Bailey West public hearing. - Oakdale Planning Commission: Thu, July 9; two public hearings scheduled. - County online comment windows (washingtoncountymn.gov): proposed Middle St. Croix Valley-to-Phalen-Keller regional trail closes July 10; County Road 10/17 intersection near Lake Elmo closes July 12. - Stillwater middle-school sports fee: the final amount is set before mid-July registration; watch the district's Community Education page. - Stuff the Bus school-supply help: register through July 23 at uwwce.org/stuff-the-bus. - Also coming up: Woodbury 2027 road-reconstruction public hearing July 22; County Board and Oakdale City Council July 14; Stillwater City Council July 21; Stillwater school board July 28.
Hosted by Artie Fishel. Sources: official city, county, and school-district agendas, meeting packets, and staff reports.
